question: Will CPI rise more than 0.8% in March 2026?
reasoning: Multiple credible sources indicate March 2026 CPI likely increased around 0.9% month-over-month due to Iran conflict driving oil prices to $120 and gasoline up $1/gallon. Cleveland Fed nowcasting projects significant inflation jump, and several economist forecasts suggest 0.8-0.9% monthly increases. While some sources show 0.3%, the weight of evidence from energy price impacts and geopolitical tensions strongly suggests the threshold will be exceeded.
key_factors: Iran conflict causing energy price surge with oil near $120/barrel
Gasoline prices up roughly $1 per gallon in March
Cleveland Fed inflation nowcasting projecting significant jump
Multiple economist forecasts suggesting 0.8-0.9% monthly increase
Energy supply disruption in Strait of Hormuz affecting global oil flows
risks: Conflicting reports showing 0.3% vs 0.9% actual results
Possible measurement timing differences between sources
Energy price volatility could reverse quickly with ceasefire
Core inflation remaining more contained than headline
Seasonal adjustment factors may affect final calculation
